creator: pgj@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hello,
 
 On Mon, Jan 7, 2013 at 8:11 AM, Hiroto Kagotani
 <hiroto.kagotani@gmail.com> wrote:
 > When dblatex has been installed, lang/ghc can not be built.
 >
 > The error message is:
 > [snip]
 > Build users_guide.ps
 > latex failed
 > users_guide.tex:3996: Undefined control sequence \Documents.
 > [snip]
 > users_guide.tex: Emergency stop.
 > Unexpected error occured
 > [ -f docs/users_guide/users_guide.ps ]
 > gmake[1]: *** [docs/users_guide/users_guide.ps] Error 1
 > [snip]
 
 Sorry, but I could not reproduce this problem.  Probably it is because
 the lang/ghc port should not build the PS/PDF version of the User's
 Guide as it is forcefully disabled in ${WRKSRC}/mk/build.mk by setting
 the value of "XMLDocWay" to "html" only.
 
 I agree that the build should not fail even when building the PS file
 but since we explicitly disable it, this has to be unrelated to
 whether textproc/dblatex is installed or not, i.e. the FreeBSD port
 has just to work.
 
 On that note, the vanilla GHC 7.4.2 FreeBSD binary tarball [1]
 contains the PDF/PS version of the User's Guide, so I assume the
 documentation sources should be fine as well.
 
 Could you please share your complete build log with us?
 
 [1] http://www.haskell.org/ghc/dist/7.4.2/ghc-7.4.2-x86_64-unknown-freebsd.tar.bz2
